title: cynoglossum capusii
intro: Cynoglossum capusii, also known as Capus's hound's tongue, is a flowering plant species in the borage family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic effects, which may help alleviate symptoms associated with these conditions, such as pain and swelling. Cynoglossum capusii can be prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is often consumed as a herbal tea or added to other herbal remedies.
